greeting: The gravedigger leans on his spade like it is furniture. "Evening. Or morning. In my trade the difference is mostly who is watching."
shrug: "Under my notice, and my notice starts six feet down."
deflect: "Some holes I dig and some I keep. You are asking after the kept kind."

entries.text: "Ezra Coombs, sexton and digger. Third generation. The dead of this town have had a Coombs see them down since before the war."
"Digging, filling, keeping the yard tidy, tolling the bell. Quiet work. The customers never complain, and the complaints never stop coming."
"Old ground up there. Older than the yard. My grandfather said never dig north of the fort fence, and he was not a man for saying things twice."

entries.deflect: "Full of dead folk, mostly." He grins with more gaps than teeth. "Mostly."
entries.text: He checks the lane both ways. "You have counted, then, or you have guessed. Aye, a few of my graves are lighter than the register says. And before you look at me sideways, the Philadelphia man pays for fresh, and these were empty when I opened them. Empty and tidy." He spits. "Somebody is withdrawing, friend, and it is not me, and they do not use a spade."
